title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article: Delta Dental and Anthem are two excellent dental insurance providers. We will also introduce you to five other reputable dental insurance providers. Feel free to take a look if you're interested.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
1. Delta Dental

Delta Dental is the largest dental insurance provider in the U.S., with a vast network of over 150,000 providers serving more than 80 million customers. They offer HMO and PPO plans, as well as managed fee-for-service and discount options. However, Delta Dental's maximum benefits are relatively low, capped at $1,000. Additionally, coverage and cost-sharing for orthodontic care are limited.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
2. Anthem Dental

Anthem provides insurance coverage in 14 states. They offer various dental insurance plans, and members can access valuable resources and digital tools. Anthem is a good choice for individuals needing standard insurance plans and coverage. It can also be bundled with health and vision policies. However, Anthem is not ideal for personalized insurance. Additionally, in 2015, the company experienced a data breach affecting millions of members.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
3. Humana Dental

Humana Dental has a large network of 260,000 dentists and serves 20 million members, with a market share of around 8%. It offers a wide range of plans, including options for veterans. AM Best rates Humana Dental's financial strength as A-. However, its coverage is somewhat limited, and not all plans and benefits are available in every state.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
4. Renaissance Dental

Renaissance Life & Health Insurance Company of America in Indiana has a large network of over 300,000 dental providers. They offer a range of excellent dental insurance plans with high-quality service. There are no waiting periods for appointments and diagnostic services. However, their annual maximum benefit is only $1,000.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
5. MetLife Dental

MetLife Dental offers various types of dental insurance plans, including employer-provided, veteran, federal, and individual plans. You have the freedom to choose in-network or out-of-network providers. As a MetLife member, you can enjoy lower costs. However, pricing information is not fully accessible online, and the customer service portal is more suited for businesses and employees rather than individual members.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
6. UnitedHealthcare

You can easily get dental insurance quotes on the UnitedHealthcare website. UnitedHealthcare dental plans cover preventive care, usually with no deductible or waiting period. The plans have no maximum age limit. However, be aware that some plans only cover major services after the first year of enrollment.

title: 7 Must-Know Dental Insurance Providers
article:
7. Cigna

Cigna is one of the top health insurance providers in the U.S. Its nationwide network covers 93,000 dentists at 287,000 locations. However, there is a 12-month waiting period for restorative and orthodontic services. Additionally, the number of plan options is limited compared to other dental insurance companies.
